description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular, slightly rounded wooden case finished in a deep reddish-brown color. The front face features a large, honeycomb-patterned speaker grille in a beige tone, occupying the upper half, with a single round knob on the left. Below the grille, there is a black control panel with two prominent round knobs on either side and a horizontal frequency indicator in the center, marked with various radio bands. The sides and back are smooth, unadorned wood, and the top is flat and polished, matching the rest of the case. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, combining both decorative and functional elements.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a honeycomb-patterned speaker grille, two control knobs, and a central frequency indicator. The case is deep reddish-brown and smoothly finished.
description_view_0: Front view showing the honeycomb-patterned speaker grille, two control knobs, and the central frequency indicator.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the smooth, unadorned wooden surface with rounded edges.
description_view_2: Top view revealing the flat, polished wooden surface without any visible controls or features.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the same smooth, rounded wooden surface as the left side.
